chapter_title.en: Chapter 195 – Regarding that the sins are three (types)

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن موثق كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (1/332)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن موثق كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (1/332)
text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ ( عليه السلام ) عَنْ رَجُلٍ أُقِيمَ عَلَيْهِ الْحَدُّ فِي الرَّجْمِ أَ يُعَاقَبُ عَلَيْهِ فِي الْآخِرَةِ قَالَ إِنَّ اللَّهَ أَكْرَمُ مِنْ ذَلِك .َ

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from Muhammad Bin Isa, from Yunus, from Ibn Bukeyr, from Zurara, from Humran who said,

‘I asked Abu Ja’farasws about a man upon whom the Legal Punishment (Hadd) is established regarding the stoning. Would there be Punishment upon him in the Hereafter?’ Heasws said: ‘Allahazwj is more Benevolent than that’ (to Punish him twice).
en.sarwar: 2. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from Muhammad ibn ‘Isa from Yunus from ibn Bukayr from Zurara from Humran who has said the following: “Once I asked abu Ja’far (a.s.) about a man who is convicted and punished by stoning to death whether he will be punished in the next life for that sin again, and the Imam said, ‘Allah is by far more Honorable, He will not do such a thing (punish again).”’
